The Python code faithfully translates all the Coq helper functions (roman_m, roman_c, roman_x, roman_i) with identical case mappings. The int_to_mini_roman_spec function checks the same range constraint (1 <= number <= 1000) and computes the expected result using the same arithmetic operations (division and modulo) to extract digits and concatenate the corresponding roman numeral parts. The Python returns a boolean indicating whether res matches the expected value, which corresponds to checking whether the Coq Prop is satisfied for concrete inputs.
